title: S3 general purpose buckets should require requests to use SSL
description: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket has a policy that requires requests to use SSL. The control fails if the bucket policy doesn't require requests to use SSL.
what_is_wrong: This control checks whether an Amazon S3 general purpose bucket has a policy that requires requests to use SSL. The control fails if the bucket policy doesn't require requests to use SSL.
what_the_fix_does: Adds policy enforcement so bucket access requires TLS/SSL.

findings.title: S3 general purpose buckets should require requests to use SSL

recommendation.rationale: Matrix cell risk_medium__criticality_high maps to default recommendation 'pr_only'. Risk is medium and business criticality is high. Criticality evidence: data_sensitivity=0.80, privilege_level=0.60, internet_exposure=0.60, matched_signals=none. Relationship context is complete enough for advisory matrix placement.

execution_guidance.label: Enforce SSL-only S3 requests (strict deny)
execution_guidance.blast_radius_summary: Targets arn:aws:s3:::config-bucket-696505809372 without intending account-wide mutation.

pre_checks.message: Confirm this change is intended for arn:aws:s3:::config-bucket-696505809372 in account 696505809372 (eu-north-1).
Confirm the repository, deployment path, and approver that own this infrastructure change before generating the PR bundle.
Provide the required strategy inputs before execution: preserve_existing_policy.
Non-TLS clients and legacy integrations will fail after strict SSL policy enforcement.
Strict SSL enforcement can conflict with existing bucket policy statements.

execution_guidance.expected_outcome: PR mode produces a reviewable infrastructure change: After applying, all HTTP (non-HTTPS) requests to this bucket will receive a 403 Forbidden response. After merge and apply through your IaC path, Security Hub should reflect the remediated state within 12-24 hours.

post_checks.message: Review the generated bundle or diff, then capture plan and apply evidence after the change is merged through the normal infrastructure workflow.
Verify the completed change only affected the intended resource scope for arn:aws:s3:::config-bucket-696505809372.
Trigger immediate re-evaluation after the change lands and confirm the action closes without reopening.

rollback.summary: Use this rollback path to revert the infrastructure change through the same reviewed delivery workflow.
notes: Prefer reverting through version control and your normal deployment pipeline so rollback stays auditable.
